patterns.summary: Vite SPA apps must wrap their render tree in an ErrorBoundary JSX element at the app root. Unlike Next.js (which provides error.tsx at route level), Vite SPA has no framework-level boundary — a missing one causes a blank screen on any unhandled render error.
bestPractices: Wrap App content in <ErrorBoundary> or <Sentry.ErrorBoundary> at the root level
Use react-error-boundary package or a custom ErrorBoundary class component
Name the boundary component with 'ErrorBoundary' in the name so the rule can detect it
Provide a fallback prop that renders a user-friendly error UI
antiPatterns: App component with JSX but no ErrorBoundary ancestor in the JSX tree
Importing ErrorBoundary but never mounting it in the JSX (T-MS-A: string grep misses this, AST check catches it)
Relying on React's default unhandled error behaviour (blank screen)

patterns.summary: React SPA components must follow web accessibility conventions enforced by eslint-plugin-jsx-a11y: interactive elements need accessible names and roles, images need alt attributes, and ARIA props must be valid.
bestPractices: Add alt attributes to all elements
Use semantic HTML elements instead of divs with click handlers
Add accessible names to interactive elements (aria-label, aria-labelledby, or visible text)
Ensure form fields have associated labels
antiPatterns: onClick on non-interactive elements without role and tabIndex

patterns.summary: React SPA components must follow the Rules of Hooks enforced by eslint-plugin-react-hooks: hooks must be called at the top level of React functions, not inside conditions, loops, or nested functions.
bestPractices: Call hooks only at the top level, never inside conditions or loops
Call hooks only from React function components or custom hooks
Name custom hooks with the 'use' prefix so the linter can identify them
Ensure all hook dependencies are declared in the exhaustive-deps rule
antiPatterns: Calling hooks inside loops or nested functions
Calling hooks from non-hook, non-component functions
Missing dependencies in useEffect/useCallback/useMemo dependency arrays
